{"notes": [{"url": "https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34167865/", "title": "Does four-week consecutive, dawn-to-sunset intermittent fasting ...", "summary": "Does four-week consecutive, dawn-to-sunset intermittent fasting during Ramadan affect cardiometabolic risk factors in healthy adults? A systematic review, meta-analysis, and meta-regression - PMID: 34167865 - DOI: 10.1016/j.numecd.2021.05.002 Does four-week consecutive, dawn-to-sunset intermittent fasting during Ramadan affect cardiometabolic risk factors in healthy adults? A systematic review, meta-analysis, and meta-regression Abstract Aims: This study aimed to evaluate the effects of Ramadan diurnal intermittent fasting (RDIF; 29-30 days) on cardiometabolic risk factors (CMRF) in healthy adults, and examine the effect of various cofactors on the outcomes using sub-group meta-regression.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["A systematic review, meta-analysis, and meta-regression - PMID: 34167865 - DOI: 10.1016/j.numecd.2021.05.002 Does four-week consecutive, dawn-to-sunset intermittent fasting during Ramadan affect cardiometabolic risk factors in healthy adults?", "A systematic review, meta-analysis, and meta-regression Abstract Aims: This study aimed to evaluate the effects of Ramadan diurnal intermittent fasting (RDIF; 29-30 days) on cardiometabolic risk factors (CMRF) in healthy adults, and examine the effec", "Ten scientific databases (EBSCOhost, CINAHL, Cochrane, EMBASE, PubMed/MEDLINE, Scopus, Google Scholar, ProQuest Medical, ScienceDirect, and Web of Science) were searched from the date of inception (1950) to the end of November 2020.", "The CMRF searched and analyzed were total cholesterol (TC), triglycerides (TG), high-density lipoprotein cholesterol (HDL-C), low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (LDL-C), very low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (VLDL-C), diastolic blood pressure (DB", "We identified 91 studies (4431 adults aged 18-85 years) conducted between 1982 and 2020 in 23 countries distributed over four continents."], "knowledge_contribution": "Adds per-page evidence on the research topic: A systematic review, meta-analysis, and meta-regression - PMID: 34167865 - DOI: 10.1016/j.numecd.2021.05.002 Does four-week consecutive, daw"}, {"url": "https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0939475321002234", "title": "Does four-week consecutive, dawn-to-sunset intermittent fasting ...", "summary": "Data synthesis We conducted a systematic review and meta-analysis to measure the effect sizes of changes in CMRF in healthy adult Muslims observing RDIF. Ten scientific databases (EBSCOhost, CINAHL, Cochrane, EMBASE, PubMed/MEDLINE, Scopus, Google Scholar, ProQuest Medical, ScienceDirect, and Web of Science) were searched from the date of inception (1950) to the end of November 2020. The CMRF searched and analyzed were total cholesterol (TC), triglycerides (TG), high-density lipoprotein cholesterol (HDL-C), low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (LDL-C), very low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (VLDL-C), diastolic blood pressure (DBP), and heart rate (HR).", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Ten scientific databases (EBSCOhost, CINAHL, Cochrane, EMBASE, PubMed/MEDLINE, Scopus, Google Scholar, ProQuest Medical, ScienceDirect, and Web of Science) were searched from the date of inception (1950) to the end of November 2020.", "The CMRF searched and analyzed were total cholesterol (TC), triglycerides (TG), high-density lipoprotein cholesterol (HDL-C), low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (LDL-C), very low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (VLDL-C), diastolic blood pressure (DB", "We identified 91 studies (4431 adults aged 18–85 years) conducted between 1982 and 2020 in 23 countries distributed over four continents.", "Meta-regression revealed that the age of fasting people was a significant moderator of changes in both HDL-C (P = 0.02) and VLDL-C (P = 0.01).", "Male sex was the only significant moderator of changes in LDL-C (P = 0.055)."], "knowledge_contribution": "Adds per-page evidence on the research topic: Ten scientific databases (EBSCOhost, CINAHL, Cochrane, EMBASE, PubMed/MEDLINE, Scopus, Google Scholar, ProQuest Medical, ScienceDirect, and "}, {"url": "https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/nutrition/articles/10.3389/fnut.2020.00028/full", "title": "Recent Evidence on the Impact of Ramadan Diurnal Intermittent ...", "summary": "We also examined the effect of their interactions on cardiometabolic risks. Furthermore, since major differences exists between Ramadan diurnal intermittent fasting compared to other forms of experimental intermittent fasting, in this article, we further restricted the discussion to Ramadan diurnal intermittent fasting. PubMed and Google Scholar databases were searched using “intermittent fasting,” “time-restricted feeding,” “fasting,” “mealtime,” “circadian rhythm,” and “cardiometabolic risk,” focusing on human studies published after 2013.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["PubMed and Google Scholar databases were searched using “intermittent fasting,” “time-restricted feeding,” “fasting,” “mealtime,” “circadian rhythm,” and “cardiometabolic risk,” focusing on human studies published after 2013.", "Recent evidence indicates that meal timing may influence circadian rhythm, as a result, it may also directly or indirectly impact cardiometabolic risk.", "Cardiometabolic risk denotes risk factors that increase the chances of developing vascular events or developing diabetes and obesity.", "Important under-recognized factors that have recently been linked to cardiometabolic risk include; intermittent fasting (IF), time-restricted feeding (TRF), mealtime, and circadian rhythm ([4](https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/nutrition/articles/1", "Disturbances of circadian rhythm disrupt body functions and may increase the risk of cardiometabolic dysfunction ([5](https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/nutrition/articles/10.3389/fnut.2020.00028/full#B5))."], "knowledge_contribution": "Adds per-page evidence on the research topic: PubMed and Google Scholar databases were searched using “intermittent fasting,” “time-restricted feeding,” “fasting,” “mealtime,” “circadian"}, {"url": "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7078334/", "title": "Recent Evidence on the Impact of Ramadan Diurnal Intermittent ...", "summary": "Abstract In this article, we reviewed recent data that examined the relationship of circadian rhythm, mealtime, and intermittent fasting with the risk of cardiometabolic dysfunction. We also examined the effect of their interactions on cardiometabolic risks. Furthermore, since major differences exists between Ramadan diurnal intermittent fasting compared to other forms of experimental intermittent fasting, in this article, we further restricted the discussion to Ramadan diurnal intermittent fasting.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["PubMed and Google Scholar databases were searched using “intermittent fasting,” “time-restricted feeding,” “fasting,” “mealtime,” “circadian rhythm,” and “cardiometabolic risk,” focusing on human studies published after 2013.", "Recent evidence indicates that meal timing may influence circadian rhythm, as a result, it may also directly or indirectly impact cardiometabolic risk.", "Cardiometabolic risk denotes risk factors that increase the chances of developing vascular events or developing diabetes and obesity.", "In this review, we mainly discussed studies published after 2013; though, relevant papers published before that date were included.", "In a well-designed study that aimed to assess the acute impact of misalignment between mealtime, sleep/wake pattern, and internal circadian rhythm on some markers of cardiometabolic health, 10 adults were subjected to a 10-day forced desynchrony prot"], "knowledge_contribution": "Adds per-page evidence on the research topic: PubMed and Google Scholar databases were searched using “intermittent fasting,” “time-restricted feeding,” “fasting,” “mealtime,” “circadian"}, {"url": "https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/british-journal-of-nutrition/article/impact-of-ramadan-diurnal-intermittent-fasting-on-the-metabolic-syndrome-components-in-healthy-nonathletic-muslim-people-aged-over-15-years-a-systematic-review-and-metaanalysis/390F52CFFD34278113368A64787DC14A", "title": "Impact of Ramadan diurnal intermittent fasting on the metabolic ...", "summary": "The metabolic syndrome (MetS) is considered a multiplex risk factor for atherosclerotic CVD and type 2 diabetes(Reference Grundy1). Major drivers of MetS are insulin resistance, atherogenic dyslipidaemia, prothrombotic state, elevated glucose, elevated blood pressure (BP), pro-inflammatory state and excess energy intake and concomitant obesity(Reference Grundy2). Mounting evidence suggests that lifestyle interventions (e.g. intermittent fasting and energetic restriction(Reference Sundfør and Svendsen3) or a weight reducing diet(Reference Harvie, Wright and Pegington4)) and lifestyle modifications (e.g. physical exercise(Reference Dalle Grave, Calugi and Centis5)) can reverse metabolic risk factors.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Mounting evidence suggests that lifestyle interventions (e.g. intermittent fasting and energetic restriction(Reference Sundfør and Svendsen3) or a weight reducing diet(Reference Harvie, Wright and Pegington4)) and lifestyle modifications (e.g. physic", "During the night hours from sunset to dawn, people are allowed to eat and drink freely but may not consume any food or drink after dawn(Reference Sakr6).", "However, no published works have discussed or systematically analysed the MetS components as a cluster of factors involved in the etiopathogenesis of the syndrome.", "This systematic review and meta-analysis aimed to systematically summarise and analyse available scientific evidence and clarify the results of published studies regarding the impact of RDIF on the MetS components among non-diabetic, non-athletic, he", "F.) conducted an electronic search on the CINAHL, Cochrane, EBSCOhost, Google Scholar, ProQuest Medical, PubMed/MEDLINE, ScienceDirect, Scopus and Web of Science databases for relevant studies published from 1950 to March 2019."], "knowledge_contribution": "Adds per-page evidence on the research topic: Mounting evidence suggests that lifestyle interventions (e.g. intermittent fasting and energetic restriction(Reference Sundfør and Svendsen3"}, {"url": "https://www.globalcardiology.info/site/article/view/32?articlesBySimilarityPage=3", "title": "The impact of diurnal fasting during Ramadan on patients with ...", "summary": "See how this article has been cited at scite.ai scite shows how a scientific paper has been cited by providing the context of the citation, a classification describing whether it supports, mentions, or contrasts the cited claim, and a label indicating in which section the citation was made. The impact of diurnal fasting during Ramadan on patients with established cardiac disease: a systematic review During the month of Ramadan, Muslims abstain from eating before dawn until after sunset for a month. This study reviews most recent literature on the effect of Ramadan fasting on the Cardiac patient specifically, excluding any study done on patients with no diagnosis of Cardiac disease to help cardiologists better deal with their patients who would wish to fast during Ramadan.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["See how this article has been cited at scite.ai scite shows how a scientific paper has been cited by providing the context of the citation, a classification describing whether it supports, mentions, or contrasts the cited claim, and a label indicatin", "Most studies have shown, with a few exceptions, that Ramadan fasting has no adverse effects on incidence of cardiovascular disease or the number of hospitalizations due to cardiac disease events.", "Altmetrics Downloads Citations How to Cite This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License.", "A 300-400 word deep analysis focused on findings relevant to the research question, including:
   - Key data points, statistics, and numerical findings
   - Methodology or approach described (if any)
   - Contradictions or tensions with commonly know", "Which STORM perspectives are covered (from: Scientific, Regulatory, Industry, Economic, Public_Health, Historical, Regional, Methodological, Emerging_Trends)
3. 3-5 specific facts with numbers, dates, or named entities
4."], "knowledge_contribution": "Adds per-page evidence on the research topic: See how this article has been cited at scite.ai scite shows how a scientific paper has been cited by providing the context of the citation, "}]}
